pages.text:
Ambassade da france

Aux Etats - Inis

APR 11

53

Caser MA Tracer 198

The Secretary of State presents his compliments to

Their Excellencies and Messieurs the Chiefs of Mission of the Governments signatory to the Treaty of Peace with

Japan and has the honor to transmit herewith two copies each of status lists, all of which are dated March 31, 1953, of the following agreements:

(1) Treaty of Peace with Japan signed at

San Francisco on September 8, 1951;

(2) Protocol opened for signature at

San Francisco on September 8, 1951; and

(3) Agreement for the Settlement of Disputes

Arising Under Article 15(a) of the

Treaty of Peace with Japan opened for signature at Washington on June 12, 1952.

It will be noted that the lists include dates of signature, dates of deposit of instruments of ratification, dates of entry into force, and other pertinent information.

The Secretary

-2-

The Secretary of State would be grateful if each

Chief of Mission would forward this information to his

Government.

Enclosures:

Three lists - two copies each.

Washington, Apr11 6, 1953.

c2B

TREATY OF PEACE WITH JAPAN

Signed at San Francisco September 8, 1951

¡Date of entry Into force: April 28, 1952 at 8:30 a.m. EST]*

TREATY OF PEACE WITH JAPAN

* The Treaty of Peace with Japan, in accordance with the provisions of Artiele 23(a) thereof, entered into force on April 28, 1952 at 8:30 a,m., Eastern Standard Time, between the Governments of

Argentina, Australia, Canada, France, Mexico, New Zealand, Pakistan, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland,

United States of America, and Japan.

The Treaty entered into force

with respect to the States subsequently depositing instruments of ratification on the date and at the time of deposit of their respective instruments of ratification.

(1) The instrument of ratification by the Government of the United

States of America contained the following declaration:

"As part of such advice and consent the Senate states that nothing the treaty contains is deemed to diminish or prejudice, in favor of the Soviet Union, the right, title, and

interest of Japan, or the

Allied Powers as

defined in said treaty, in and to

South Sakhalin and its adjacent islands, the Kurile

Islands, the Habomal Islands, the island of Shikotan, or any other territory, rights, or interests possessed by Japan on December 7, title, or benefet therein or theret contre so tet

or to confer any right,

Union; and also that nothing in the said treaty, or the advice and consent of the Senate to the ratifi- cation thereof, implies recognition on the part of the United States of the provisions in favor of the

Soviet Union contained in the so-called 'Yalta agree- ment' regarding Japan of February 11, 1945."

(2) The instrument of ratification by the Government of El

Salvador contained the following declarations:

"I) El Salvador no acepta ni ratifica los compromisos

que otros Estados puedan haber contraido con respecto a la transferencia o final disposición de los territorios enumerados en el articulo

Segundo del Tratado de Paz con el Japón, en todos aquellos casos en que no se haya consultado y acatado la voluntad 11bremente expresada por las poblaciones afectadas.

Esta reserva se aplica a la transferencia de las Islas Kuriles, de una parte de la Isla

Sa jaline (Sakhalin) y a cualauler otro caso analogo, incluyendo además todo compromiso sobre la Isla de Formosa; y

"II) No podrá tener efecto alguno en El Salvador, debido

a que son contrarias a su Constitución Política, las disposiciones del Artículo XIV del Tratado de Paz con el Japón que autorizan la confiscación de blenes de personas naturales o jurídicas japonesas,"

Prepared by

Treaty Affairs,
